step_by_step_thinking: The traditional technique described here is prone to inaccuracies and limitations related to the shape of the file itself rather than being able to effectively address all aspects of canal preparation. Furthermore, it struggles with removing accessory canals adequately, leading to potential issues like incomplete cleaning and poor sealing outcomes post-obturation. Therefore, although efficient and relatively easy-to-use, the risks associated make it less commonly utilized compared to newer, more refined techniques.
